title: Kachumbari

overview.description: A fresh East African salad of tomatoes, red onion, coriander, lime and chilli, served with avocado and beans for a more substantial meal.

ingredients: 1 x 400g tin black beans or kidney beans, drained and rinsed
½ tsp salt, or to taste
method: Place the diced red onion in a bowl.
Cover with cold water and leave for 10 minutes.
Add the tomatoes, avocado, beans, coriander and chilli if using.
Add the lime juice, olive oil, salt and black pepper.
Taste and adjust the lime juice and seasoning.

learning.did-you-know: The name kachumbari is related to the Swahili word for a salad of chopped fresh ingredients. It is particularly popular across East Africa and is commonly served with grilled meats and rice dishes.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How a simple combination of fresh ingredients can be balanced through acidity, salt and heat, and how to turn a traditional accompaniment into a more substantial meal.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick, inexpensive and highly adaptable salad that introduces East African flavours and can work either as a complete bean-based meal or as a fresh accompaniment to grilled foods.
chapman-tips: Soaking the diced onion briefly in cold water softens its sharpness.
Add the avocado shortly before serving so it remains intact.
The lime should provide a noticeable sharpness.
Chilli is optional and can be added only to adult portions.
For the most traditional accompaniment, serve the core tomato and onion salad alongside grilled meat.
Beans make the salad much more substantial as a complete meal.
Chapati is an excellent accompaniment and makes the meal more filling.
notes.batch-cooking: Better fresh. The tomatoes release liquid and the avocado softens over time.
notes.leftovers: Best eaten the same day. Leftovers can be used in wraps or served with rice and beans.
